architecture.attention_pattern: hybrid: sliding-window local attention alternating with full global attention; 5:1 local:global ratio across 30 layers; pattern repeats every 5 layers with 1 global layer
architecture.vision_encoder: ~550M params, 27 layers, hidden_size=1152; multimodal (image+text); not relevant to text-only GGUF inference

recommended_sampling.notes: Google official defaults: temp=1.0, top_p=0.95, top_k=64, confirmed by Unsloth docs and llama.cpp discussions. min_p=0 (disabled) per community usage. Sampler order matters in llama.cpp — explicitly pass --samplers 'temperature;top_p;top_k'. Some users report temp=1.5 improves coding tasks but 1.0 is the official baseline. No repetition_penalty documented by Google.

recommended_context.yarn_notes: Native 256K context via dual RoPE (standard for sliding layers, pruned for global layers). No YaRN extension required or documented. Sliding-window layers only need last 1024 tokens in KV cache — full 256K KV cost applies only to global attention layers. llama.cpp SWA KV cache compression (ik_llama.cpp issue #1607) is an active optimization area but not yet mainstream.

chat_template.system_prompt_notes: Gemma 4 adds NATIVE system role support (unlike Gemma 1/2/3 which folded system into first user turn). Use the model's built-in chat template via processor.apply_chat_template() or llama.cpp's --chat-template gemma. Do NOT manually construct prompts.
chat_template.notes: Gemma 4 is multimodal; text-only GGUF variants drop the vision encoder. Images go before text, audio after text in multimodal turn ordering. System role is natively supported — a significant change from earlier Gemma generations.

thinking.how: Pass <|think|> control token in system prompt to enable reasoning mode. Use llama-server (not llama-cli) for reliable thinking control.
thinking.recommended: Disable for benchmarking unless testing reasoning capability. llama.cpp flag: --chat-template-kwargs '{"enable_thinking":false}' (PowerShell: --chat-template-kwargs "{\"enable_thinking\":false}")

llama_cpp_notes: Sliding-window attention (window=1024) means local layers only need 1024-token KV window — significantly reduces KV cache memory vs full-attention models at large contexts. Global attention layers still require full KV. Use llama-server over llama-cli for thinking/template control. Recommended flags: --temp 1.0 --top-p 0.95 --top-k 64 --samplers 'temperature;top_p;top_k'. For vision (mmproj files): --mmproj <path> — not applicable to text-only GGUF builds. Architecture is gemma4 in llama.cpp (distinct from gemma3).

finetune_overrides.description: Uncensored abliterated finetune; no documented sampler overrides in model card. Card shows -c 8192 usage example but this is not a recommended max — base model supports 256K. Use base sampling settings.

Google official QAT (Quantization-Aware Training) weights converted to GGUF Q4_0 by LM Studio team. QAT integrates quantization during training rather than post-hoc — Google claims near-BF16 quality at Q4_0 precision. However, Unsloth benchmarks show naive BF16→Q4_0 GGUF conversion loses significant quality (70.2% top-1 accuracy vs 85.6% with proper lattice alignment). Verify whether lmstudio-community used Google's native QAT GGUF release or re-converted from BF16. File size ~14.4GB.

sources.role: official model card — architecture, context, system prompt support
GGUF model card — sampling params, context, sliding window, architecture details from config.json
Unsloth official docs — confirmed temp=1.0/top_p=0.95/top_k=64, llama.cpp flags, thinking toggle
QAT quality analysis — naive Q4_0 accuracy loss, Unsloth UD-Q4_K_XL recommendation
Community discussion — sampler order importance, min_p=0 confirmation, coding temp notes
HF Gemma 4 launch blog — context windows, SWA sizes, MoE active params, chat template guidance
SWA KV cache compression issue — confirms SWA KV optimization opportunity for Gemma 4

gaps: 1) Exact local:global attention layer indices not confirmed (5:1 ratio from community analysis, not official doc). 2) TrevorJS uncensored card is thin — no sampler overrides documented, can only inherit base. 3) lmstudio-community QAT conversion fidelity vs Google native QAT GGUF unclear — Q4_0 via naive conversion may have accuracy penalty per Unsloth research. 4) repetition_penalty: no Google recommendation found; likely not needed given temp=1.0 with top_k=64. 5) Vision encoder GGUF compatibility in text-only builds needs per-file verification.
